content: The original Self-Refine framework, which relies on single-perspective iterative self-feedback to improve outputs without extra training, can be enhanced by integrating the multiple inconsistent perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to break the "feedback loop bias" that occurs when an LLM only critiques its own work from its original entrenched reasoning frame. This modification would let Self-Refine retain its core advantage of requiring no additional supervised data or training while fixing its key limitation of repeating the same hidden flaws in every iteration of self-feedback.
